question:
Consider the following statements regarding the mega hydro power projects approved by the CCEA in Arunachal Pradesh in April 2026:

1. The combined investment for the projects is Rs. 40,175.33 crore.
2. The projects include the 1200 MW Kalai-II HEP and the 1720 MW Kamala HEP.
3. The Kalai-II HEP will be developed on the Lohit River in Anjaw District.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: As per the Current Affairs Pocket PDF April 2026 (Page 6), all statements are true. The CCEA approved two mega hydroelectric projects in Arunachal Pradesh with a combined investment of Rs. 40,175.33 crore. They comprise the 1200 MW Kalai-II HEP on the Lohit River and the 1720 MW Kamala HEP.

question:
Match the following Union Cabinet approvals from April 2026 with their corresponding details:

Column I:
1. NBS rates for Kharif Season 2026
2. Jaipur Metro Phase-2
3. HPCL Rajasthan Refinery Limited (HRRL)
4. Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool

Column II:
A. Sovereign guarantee of Rs. 12,980 crore
B. Estimated cost of Rs. 13,037.66 crore
C. Budgetary allocation of Rs. 41,533.81 crore
D. Revised project cost to Rs. 79,459 Crore

Select the correct answer:
rationale: As per Page 6, NBS rates have an allocation of Rs. 41,533.81 crore; Jaipur Metro Phase-2 costs Rs. 13,037.66 crore; HRRL project cost was revised to Rs. 79,459 crore; and Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool has a sovereign guarantee of Rs. 12,980 crore.

question: The Government of Andhra Pradesh launched a free electricity scheme for handloom and powerloom weavers from 1 April 2026. What is the annual outlay of this scheme?
rationale: As per Page 7, the AP Government launched a free electricity scheme for handloom and power loom weavers with an annual outlay of Rs. 150 crore.

question: Which of the following languages were approved as official state languages alongside English by the Meghalaya Cabinet in April 2026?
rationale: As per Page 7, the Meghalaya Cabinet approved the 'Meghalaya Official Languages Ordinance, 2026', declaring Khasi and Garo as official languages alongside English.

question:
Read the following statements and identify the correct combination:

Fact 1: The Puducherry election authorities deployed a saree-clad humanoid robot named 'Nila' to welcome voters at a polling booth.
Fact 2: Telangana’s AI hub Aikam signed an MoU with Deakin University, Australia, to boost AI innovation.

Which of the following is correct?

options.C: Both Fact 1 and Fact 2 are correct
options.D: Both Fact 1 and Fact 2 are incorrect
options.E: Fact 1 is correct but relates to Kerala

rationale: As per Page 8, both statements are correct. Puducherry deployed the robot 'Nila', and Telangana's Aikam signed an MoU with Deakin University.

question: Regarding the 'Nyaya Setu' AI Chatbot unveiled at the DISHA programme in New Delhi, which of the following statements is NOT correct?

options.A: It was unveiled by Vice-President C.P. Radhakrishnan.
options.B: The mascot of the chatbot is named 'Dishika'.
options.C: It was organised by the Department of Justice under the Ministry of Law & Justice.
options.D: BHASHINI's 'Shrutlekh' system was showcased for real-time transcription.
options.E: The chatbot is specifically designed exclusively for supreme court judges to draft judgments.

rationale: As per Page 8, Mascot 'Dishika' serves as a friendly digital interface that guides users through legal queries. It is not exclusively for judges to draft judgments.

question: The Ministry of Education (MoE) in collaboration with the CBSE launched a new curriculum on Computational Thinking (CT) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) for Classes ________ to be implemented from the 2026–27 academic session.
rationale: As per Page 8, MoE and CBSE launched a new curriculum on Computational Thinking and AI for Classes 3 to 8.

question: Based on the inaugural Annual Survey of Incorporated Services Sector Enterprises (ASISSE) launched by NSO, what is the reference period for the data collection?
rationale: As per Page 10, the newly launched survey covers incorporated services sector enterprises, with the reference period being the Financial Year 2024-25 (FY25).

question:
Match the findings from the NITI Aayog report “From Borrowers to Builders: Women and India’s Evolving Credit Market” (2nd edition):

Column I:
1. Total credit portfolio held by women
2. Share in total system credit
3. Credit penetration among women in 2025
4. Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of credit-active borrowers

Column II:
A. 26%
B. 36%
C. Rs. 76 lakh crore
D. 9%

Select the correct code:
rationale: As per Page 10, women hold a credit portfolio of Rs.76 lakh crore (1-C), accounting for 26% of total system credit (2-A). Credit penetration reached 36% in 2025 (3-B), and credit-active borrowers are growing at a 9% CAGR (4-D).

question:
Arrange the following Small Savings Schemes in descending order based on their interest rates for Q1FY27 (Highest to Lowest):

1. Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
2. Public Provident Fund (PPF)
3. 1-Year Post Office Time Deposits (POTD)
4. Post Office Savings Deposit (POSD)

Select the correct answer:
rationale: As per Page 24, SSY = 8.2%, PPF = 7.1%, 1-Year POTD = 6.9%, POSD = 4.0%. Hence, the descending order is 1, 2, 3, 4.

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 3rd edition of the AICTE-VAANI scheme:

1. It aims to promote Indian languages in technical education.
2. The scheme allocates an annual outlay of Rs. 4 crore to support 200 conferences and seminars.
3. Events under the scheme are conducted in 22 Indian languages listed under the Eighth Schedule.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: As per Page 11, all the statements are correct. AICTE-VAANI stands for Vibrant Advocacy for Advancement and Nurturing of Indian Languages, allocating Rs. 4 crore for 200 events in 22 Indian languages.

question: The Ministry of Textiles launched 'Vishwa Sutra – Weaves of India for the World', showcasing 30 traditional weaves at the 61st Femina Miss India 2026 held in ________.
rationale: As per Page 11, the Ministry of Textiles launched 'Vishwa Sutra' at the 61st Femina Miss India 2026 in Bhubaneswar, Odisha.

question: Which state hosted the groundbreaking ceremony of India’s first advanced 3D glass semiconductor packaging unit at Info Valley in April 2026?
rationale: As per Page 11, Odisha hosted the groundbreaking ceremony for India’s first advanced glass semiconductor packaging unit at Info Valley, Khordha district, Bhubaneswar.

question:
Match the newly designated UNESCO Global Geoparks (2026) with their respective countries:

Column I:
1. Changshan
2. Terres d’Hérault
3. Nisyros
4. Dahar

Column II:
A. Greece
B. Tunisia
C. China
D. France

Select the correct answer:
rationale: As per Page 26-27, Changshan is in China (1-C), Terres d’Hérault is in France (2-D), Nisyros is in Greece (3-A), and Dahar is in Tunisia (4-B).

question: Which of the following statements is NOT correct regarding the 4th India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS-IV)?

options.A: The logo features a Lion superimposed on interlocked maps of India and Africa.
options.B: The theme is "IA SPIRIT: India-Africa Strategic Partnership for Innovation, Resilience, and Inclusive Transformation".
options.C: The official website launched is www.iafs2026.in.
options.D: This summit marks the renewal of ties after the third summit held in 2020.
options.E: It was officially launched by Union Minister Dr. S. Jaishankar.

rationale: As per Page 12, the event marks a season of renewal for India-Africa ties after the third summit held in 2015, not 2020.

question:
Consider the following pairs of recent inaugurations and their locations:

1. India’s first alkaline battery unit by Eveready - Jammu
2. X-Band Doppler Weather Radar by IITM - Mahabaleshwar, Maharashtra
3. India’s first Petroglyph Conservation Park - Leh, Ladakh

Which of the pairs given above is/are matched correctly?
rationale: As per Page 13 & 21, Eveready commissioned India’s first alkaline battery unit in Jammu; IITM Pune inaugurated a Doppler Weather Radar at Mahabaleshwar; and India’s first Petroglyph Conservation Park was set up in Leh, Ladakh.

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 4th tranche of the Electronics Components Manufacturing Scheme (ECMS) approved in March 2026:

1. The MeitY approved 29 proposals with a projected investment of Rs 7,104 crore.
2. The total expected direct employment generation from these 29 projects is 14,246.
3. Major investments include Rs 1,683 crore in capital goods and Rs 1,350 crore in display module manufacturing.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: As per Page 14, MeitY approved 29 proposals under the 4th tranche of ECMS with an investment of Rs 7,104 crore, generating 14,246 direct jobs. Major investments include Rs 1,683 crore in capital goods and Rs 1,350 crore in display modules.

question: The Ministry of Heavy Industries extended the demand subsidy for electric two-wheelers under the PM E-DRIVE Scheme till ________.
rationale: As per Page 14, the Ministry of Heavy Industries extended the demand subsidy for electric two-wheelers under the PM E-DRIVE Scheme till July 31, 2026.

question:
Arrange the following Top 4 airports globally according to the Skytrax World Airport Awards 2026 in descending order (from Rank 1 to 4):

1. Tokyo Haneda International Airport
2. Singapore Changi Airport
3. Hong Kong International Airport
4. Incheon International Airport

Select the correct sequence:
rationale: As per Page 70, the World’s Top 4 Best Airports are: 1. Singapore Changi Airport, 2. Incheon International Airport, 3. Tokyo Haneda International Airport, 4. Hong Kong International Airport.

question:
Read the following factual statements from National Affairs:

Fact 1: The MoEFCC designated the Bhavasagara Referral Centre at CMLRE, Kochi as India’s first National Repository for Deep-Sea Fauna.
Fact 2: India has emerged as the global leader in issuing Internationally Recognised Certificates of Compliance (IRCCs) under the Nagoya Protocol.

Which of the following is correct?

options.C: Both Fact 1 and Fact 2 are correct
options.D: Both Fact 1 and Fact 2 are incorrect
options.E: Fact 1 is correct but the repository is in Mumbai

rationale: As per Page 14-15, both statements are correct. MoEFCC designated the Bhavasagara Referral Centre in Kochi as India's first National Repository for Deep-Sea Fauna, and India leads globally in issuing IRCCs under the Nagoya Protocol.

question:
Match the State Government schemes launched in 2026 with their respective states:

Column I:
1. Mukh Mantri Mawan Dheeyan Satkar Yojana
2. CM-INSPIRE scheme for UPSC Qualifiers
3. 10-year 'Warranty' Model for Rural Water Schemes under JJM

Column II:
A. Uttar Pradesh
B. Punjab
C. Meghalaya

Select the correct answer:
rationale: As per Page 25, the Mukh Mantri Mawan Dheeyan Satkar Yojana is from Punjab (1-B), CM-INSPIRE is from Meghalaya (2-C), and the 10-year Warranty Model for JJM is from UP (3-A).

question: According to the Television (TV) Rating Policy (TRP 2026) notified by MIB, which of the following is NOT correct?

options.A: The policy replaces the existing 2014 guidelines.
options.B: Any viewership arising out of a landing page will be excluded in viewership measurement.
options.C: The minimum net worth requirement for a TV rating agency has been increased from Rs 5 crore to Rs 20 crore.
options.D: TV rating agencies are required to scale up operations to 80,000 metered homes within 18 months.
options.E: The long-term goal for the sample size is 1.20 lakh metered homes.

rationale: As per Page 16, MIB has EASED the minimum net worth requirement for a TV rating agency from the existing Rs 20 crore to Rs 5 crore. Thus, stating it was increased is incorrect.

question: On April 6, 2026, India’s 1st indigenously developed ________ Megawatt electric (MWe) Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at Kalpakkam successfully achieved first criticality.
rationale: As per Page 16, India's 1st indigenously developed 500 MWe Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at Kalpakkam achieved first criticality.

question: Union Minister Jayant Chaudhary initiated a campaign to establish the world’s largest Outcomes-Based Financing (OBF) initiative. What is the name of this fund?
rationale: As per Page 17, Jayant Chaudhary initiated a campaign to establish the Skills Outcomes Fund (SOF) to enhance employment opportunities for youth.

question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's decision on the UN Climate Summit:

1. India officially withdrew its proposal to host the COP33 Climate Summit in 2028.
2. Following India's withdrawal, Japan remains as the sole bidder for the 2028 summit.
3. The withdrawal letter cited a 'review of its commitments'.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: As per Page 17, India withdrew its proposal for COP33, leaving South Korea (not Japan) as the sole bidder. The letter cited a 'review of its commitments'.

question:
Match the following National Affairs developments with their relevant authorities/states:

Column I:
1. Approval for Warangal & Adilabad Airports
2. Approval for PPFAS AMC to sponsor a pension fund
3. Development of 'Arogya Van'
4. Constitution in Sindhi Language release

Column II:
A. NHAI
B. Telangana
C. Vice President C. P. Radhakrishnan
D. PFRDA

Select the correct answer:
rationale: As per Page 18, Warangal & Adilabad Airports are in Telangana (1-B); PFRDA approved PPFAS AMC (2-D); NHAI developed Arogya Van (3-A); VP C. P. Radhakrishnan released the Constitution in Sindhi Language (4-C).

question:
According to the Tracxn 'India Tech Annual Funding Report 2026', arrange the Top 4 countries by technology startup funding in descending order (highest to lowest):

1. United States of America
2. China
3. United Kingdom
4. India

Select the correct sequence:
rationale: As per Page 71, the Top 4 countries by funding are: 1. USA (USD 426B), 2. UK (USD 17.8B), 3. China (USD 15.0B), 4. India (USD 11.7B). Hence the order is USA, UK, China, India (1, 3, 2, 4).

question:
Identify the correct statements regarding recent MeitY and MoC&I notifications in April 2026:

1. India’s first chip fabrication plant, a Tata Group subsidiary, is notified at Dholera SEZ, Gujarat with a Rs 91,000 crore investment.
2. MeitY constituted the Artificial Intelligence Governance and Economic Group (AIGEG), chaired by Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw.
3. Startup India Fund of Funds 2.0 (FoF 2.0) was notified with a total corpus of Rs 10,000 crore.

Select the correct code:
rationale: As per Page 19, all three statements are true. Dholera SEZ got the Rs 91,000 crore Tata chip plant; AIGEG was formed under Ashwini Vaishnaw; and FoF 2.0 was established with Rs 10,000 crore.

question:
Consider the following power and energy targets set by the Government of India:

Fact 1: The Central Electricity Authority (CEA) announced a target to increase India's nuclear power capacity to 100 GW by 2047.
Fact 2: India's total installed wind power capacity crossed 56 GW in FY26, supporting the target of 500 GW non-fossil fuel capacity by 2030.

Which of the following is correct?

options.E: Fact 1 is true but the target year is 2030

rationale: As per Pages 16 & 20, India added a record 6.05 GW wind power to cross 56 GW total capacity, aiming for 500 GW non-fossil by 2030. Further, CEA targeted 100 GW nuclear power by 2047.

question: Regarding India's first intra-city drone delivery flight trials in April 2026, which of the following is NOT correct?

options.A: The trials were successfully completed in Bengaluru, Karnataka.
options.B: It was a collaboration between FedEx and IIT-Madras.
options.C: It is part of international efforts aimed at developing safe and scalable logistics.
options.D: The service is managed exclusively by the Indian Air Force.
options.E: Both A and B are correct statements.

rationale: As per Page 21, the initiative is a collaboration between Federal Express Corporation (FedEx) and IIT-Madras, completing trials in Bengaluru. It is not managed by the Indian Air Force.

question: In April 2026, the Government of India approved a ________ billion currency swap facility for the Maldives under the SAARC Currency Swap Framework.
rationale: As per Page 22, the Government of India approved a Rs. 30 billion currency swap facility for the Maldives under the SAARC Currency Swap Framework.

question: Under the pilot phase of the Prime Minister Internship Scheme (PMIS), the eligibility criteria were expanded in April 2026 to include which of the following age brackets?
rationale: As per Page 21, the MCA expanded eligibility to include final-year undergraduate and postgraduate students aged 18–25, revising the earlier 21–24 age bracket.

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the newly commissioned Himadri Speciality Chemical Limited plant in West Bengal:

1. It is India’s first anode material production facility.
2. It has an initial capacity of 200 Metric Tonnes Per Annum (MTPA).
3. The facility marks a major entry into the lithium-ion battery value chain.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: As per Page 23, Himadri Speciality Chemical commissioned India’s first anode material production facility in West Bengal with an initial capacity of 200 MTPA, making a major entry into the lithium-ion battery value chain.

question: In April 2026, Ladakh Lieutenant Governor officially notified the creation of five new districts. Which of the following is NOT one of the newly created districts?
rationale: As per Page 23, the 5 new districts are Nubra, Sham, Changthang, Zanskar, and Drass. Kargil was already an existing district along with Leh.

question:
Based on the IMF's April 2026 World Economic Outlook (WEO) report, arrange the Top 4 Global Economies projected for 2025 (Nominal GDP) in descending order:

1. China
2. Japan
3. Germany
4. United States of America

Select the correct sequence:
rationale: As per Page 72, the top 4 economies for 2025 are: 1. USA (USD 39T), 2. China (USD 27.5T), 3. Germany (USD 5T), 4. Japan (USD 4.4T).

question: In April 2026, the Permanent Mission of India announced that India was elected by acclamation to three subsidiary bodies of the UN ECOSOC, which include CSTD, NGO Committee, and ________.
rationale: As per Page 26, India was elected to three UN ECOSOC bodies: CSTD, NGO Committee, and the Committee for Programme and Coordination (CPC).

question:
Consider the following pairs of diplomatic visits to India in April 2026 and the respective leader:

1. Denis Manturov - First Deputy Prime Minister of Russia
2. Christian Stocker - Federal Chancellor of Austria
3. Lee Jae Myung - President of South Korea

Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched?
rationale: As per Pages 27-28, Denis Manturov is the First Deputy PM of Russia, Christian Stocker is the Federal Chancellor of Austria, and Lee Jae Myung is the President of South Korea. All visited India in April 2026.

question:
Read the following statements regarding Vice President C.P. Radhakrishnan's official visit to Sri Lanka in April 2026:

Fact 1: This marks the first bilateral visit by an Indian Vice President to Sri Lanka.
Fact 2: During the visit, Sri Lanka agreed to join the International Big Cat Alliance (IBCA).

Which of the following is correct?

options.E: Fact 1 is correct but relates to Nepal

rationale: As per Page 29, it was indeed the first bilateral visit by an Indian Vice President to Sri Lanka, and Sri Lanka agreed to join the International Big Cat Alliance (IBCA).

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the RBI's State Borrowing Plan for Q1 FY27:

1. State governments and UTs are set to collectively raise Rs 2,54,509 crore.
2. Nine states will participate in the BIS pilot and are projected to borrow Rs 1,53,900 crore.
3. The remaining states and UTs will raise funds exclusively through international bonds.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: As per Page 30, statements 1 and 2 are true. Statement 3 is incorrect as the remaining states and UTs will raise Rs 1,00,609 crore through traditional market borrowing methods, not exclusively through international bonds.

question: According to the RBI’s 1st Bi-Monthly Monetary Policy of FY27, which of the following policy rates is NOT matched correctly?
rationale: As per Page 31, the Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) is maintained at 3.00%, not 4.50%.

question:
Match the banks/financial institutions with the penalty imposed by RBI in April 2026:

Column I:
1. Airtel Payments Bank
2. Bandhan Bank
3. Muthoot Housing Finance

Column II:
A. Rs 41.8 lakh
B. Rs 31.80 lakh
C. Rs 80,000

Select the correct answer:
rationale: As per Page 30 & 33, Airtel Payments Bank was penalized Rs 31.80 lakh (1-B), Bandhan Bank was penalized Rs 41.8 lakh (2-A), and Muthoot Housing Finance was penalized Rs 80,000 (3-C).

question: SEBI launched three new IT platforms in April 2026. The platform named ________ replaces traditional email-based communication with SEBI, ensuring centrally accessible interactions.
rationale: As per Page 33-34, SUPCOMS 2.1 replaces traditional email-based communication with SEBI, ensuring all interactions are centrally accessible.

question: In April 2026, World Bank Group President Ajay Banga launched the 'Water Forward' initiative to improve water security for 1 billion people by which year?
rationale: As per Page 36-37, the 'Water Forward' initiative aims to improve water security for 1 billion people by the year 2030.

question:
According to the SIPRI Fact Sheet April 2026, arrange the top 4 countries by military spending in 2025 in descending order:

1. Russia
2. China
3. United States of America
4. Germany

Select the correct sequence:
rationale: As per Page 73, the top spenders are USA (1st), China (2nd), Russia (3rd), and Germany (4th). Thus, the correct arrangement is 3, 2, 1, 4.

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the GDP growth projections for India for FY27 released in April 2026:

1. Moody’s Ratings lowered India’s GDP growth forecast to 6%.
2. Asian Development Bank (ADB) raised the forecast to 6.9%.
3. State Bank of India (SBI) projected a growth rate of 6.8%–7.1%.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: As per Pages 39-41, Moody's forecast is 6%, ADB is 6.9%, and SBI is 6.8%-7.1%. All statements are correct.

question:
Match the organization with the entity they signed an MoU with in April 2026:

Column I:
1. Central Council for Research in Ayurvedic Sciences (CCRAS)
2. Financial Intelligence Unit, India (FIU-IND)
3. Doordarshan (DD)

Column II:
A. Indian Cyber Crime Coordination Centre (I4C)
B. Indian Council for Cultural Relations (ICCR)
C. Anuvadini AI

Select the correct answer:
rationale: As per Pages 45-48, CCRAS signed an MoU with Anuvadini AI (1-C), FIU-IND signed with I4C (2-A), and Doordarshan signed with ICCR (3-B).

question: Identify the incorrect pairing of recent appointments made in April 2026:

options.A: Vir Vikram Yadav - Director General of Civil Aviation (DGCA)
options.B: William Walsh - Chief Executive Officer of IndiGo
options.C: Samrat Choudhary - Chief Minister of Bihar
options.D: Vibha Padalkar - Managing Director & CEO of HDFC Bank

rationale: As per Page 55, Vibha Padalkar was reappointed as the MD & CEO of HDFC Life Insurance Company Limited, not HDFC Bank.

question: In April 2026, Tô Lâm, General Secretary of the Communist Party of Vietnam (CPV), was unanimously re-elected as the President of ________.
rationale: As per Page 56, Tô Lâm was unanimously re-elected as the President of Vietnam.

question:
Consider the following pairs of awards and their recipients from April 2026:

1. 9th Prof. M.S. Swaminathan Award - Dr. Ch. Srinivasa Rao
2. South Asian ‘Person of the Year’ - Sayani Gupta
3. International Hall of Fame of USAWC - General Upendra Dwivedi

Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched?
rationale: As per Pages 60-62, all pairs are correctly matched. Dr. Ch. Srinivasa Rao won the Swaminathan award, Sayani Gupta was named South Asian 'Person of the Year', and General Upendra Dwivedi was inducted into the USAWC Hall of Fame.

question:
Read the following statements regarding the military exercises conducted in April 2026:

Fact 1: The fourth edition of the India-Egypt Joint Special Forces Exercise 'Cyclone-IV' commenced in Anshas, Egypt.
Fact 2: The seventh edition of the India–Uzbekistan Joint Exercise 'DUSTLIK 2026' was held in Gurumsaray, Uzbekistan.

Which of the following is correct?

options.E: Fact 1 is true but Fact 2 relates to Kazakhstan

rationale: As per Page 77, both statements are true. Cyclone-IV was held in Anshas, Egypt, and DUSTLIK 2026 was held in Namangan/Gurumsaray, Uzbekistan.
